qa_pairs.question: How are the Streaming Multiprocessors (SMs) of an H100 SXM5 GPU typically partitioned when using Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) technology?
qa_pairs.answer: Under Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) technology, an H100 SXM5 GPU's 132 Streaming Multiprocessors (SMs) are partitioned into one instance of 60 SMs (nvidia_h100_80gb_hbm3_3g.40gb), one instance of 32 SMs (nvidia_h100_80gb_hbm3_2g.20gb), and two instances of 16 SMs (nvidia_h100_80gb_hbm3_1g.10gb). This configuration leaves 8 SMs unassigned and effectively unused.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it more precise to describe MIG partitioning of an H100 GPU in terms of 'thirty-thirdths' rather than 'eighths' as often used by NVIDIA?
qa_pairs.answer: It is more precise to describe MIG partitioning of an H100 GPU in terms of 'thirty-thirdths' because the H100 SXM5 GPU has 132 Streaming Multiprocessors (SMs), which are not evenly divisible by eight. While NVIDIA might refer to 'one eighth,' this actually corresponds to 4/33 of an H100 GPU, 'two eighths' to 8/33, and 'three eighths' to 15/33, with 2/33 of the GPU's total capacity remaining unassigned after partitioning into instances of 60, 32, and two 16 SMs.
